messages.content:
[Model: anthropic/claude-opus-4.6]

[Provider: OpenRouter]

You are an expert full-stack engineer. Build a production-quality web app called “FishHunt” for a UK teenager and his friends. It must be impressive, fast, and simple to use.

TECH STACK (STRICT)
Backend: Laravel (latest stable), PHP 8.3
Database: PostgreSQL
Frontend: Blade + Alpine.js (no React/Vue), Tailwind CSS for styling
Auth: Laravel Breeze (Blade) or equivalent Blade-based auth scaffolding
Storage: local disk (public) for uploaded images; include a clear path to switch to S3 later
Use Laravel policies + gates for permissions, and database-backed roles/permissions (do not hardcode)

CORE FEATURES (MUST HAVE)

1) Authentication + Roles/Permissions
Users can register/login/logout/password reset.
Roles: Admin, Moderator, Member.
Permissions example:
  - Admin: manage users, roles, site settings, delete any post/hunt
  - Moderator: remove posts/comments, approve/deny reported content
  - Member: create posts, create hunts, comment, like
Implement:
  - roles table, permissions table, pivot tables
  - middleware: role/permission checks
  - admin UI to assign roles and manage permissions

2) Photo Upload + Public Feed (Fishing Activity)
Users can create a “Catch Post”:
  - Title, description, location (text + optional lat/lng), species (optional), bait used (optional), gear notes (optional), date/time caught
  - Upload 1–5 images (validate type/size; generate thumbnails)
Public feed page:
  - Infinite scroll or “Load more”
  - Filters: species, location, date range, “near me” (if lat/lng present)
  - Post detail page with gallery
  - Like button + comment thread
Moderation:
  - Report post/comment
  - Moderator/Admin can hide/delete content
Privacy:
  - Location can be “Exact” or “Approximate” (obfuscate by rounding coordinates if approximate)

3) “Hunt” Checklist Planner (Impressive Feature)
Users can create a Hunt Plan with:
Location (name + optional lat/lng + country/region)
Date/time window (start/end)
Notes / rough plan text
When a hunt is created, the app generates a checklist and “Hunt Briefing” by pulling together:
A) Weather forecast for location/date (from a public API)
B) Tides for coastal locations (public API)
C) Rainfall / precipitation probability (from weather API)
D) Species likely at that location for the time of year (public sources)
E) Suggested bait per species
F) Suggested rods/line/tackle/equipment
G) “Timing tips” (best time of day, tide state suggestions if coastal)
This output should be displayed as:
A neat briefing card
A tickable checklist (persisted per hunt)
A shareable link to show mates (read-only public view, optionally)

IMPORTANT: API INTEGRATION APPROACH
Use environment variables for API keys.
Provide a “Demo Mode” fallback: if no API keys are set, seed realistic sample data so the feature still works.
Use queued jobs for external API calls (so the UI stays snappy), and cache results.
Implement a provider abstraction layer:
  - WeatherProviderInterface
  - TideProviderInterface
  - SpeciesInfoProviderInterface
So providers can be swapped later.

DATA MODEL (MINIMUM)
users
roles, permissions, role_user, permission_role (or permission_user if needed)
posts
post_images
comments
likes
reports
hunts
hunt_checklist_items
hunt_briefings (cached generated briefing JSON + timestamps + provider metadata)

UX / “IMPRESS HIS MATES” REQUIREMENTS
Home page shows a bold feed with image cards, likes/comments count, and a “Create Post” button.
Profile page shows user’s catches + hunts, plus simple badges (e.g., “First Catch”, “3 Species Logged”, “5 Hunts Planned”).
Hunt page shows:
  - location map preview if lat/lng exists (use a lightweight map embed; keep dependencies minimal)
  - clear “Hunt Briefing” section
  - checklist with satisfying tick animations (Alpine.js)
Make everything mobile-first.

SECURITY / QUALITY
Validate all inputs.
Authorisation via policies for posts/comments/hunts.
Rate-limit uploads and posting.
Sanitize displayed text.
Use database transactions where needed.
Add seeders with demo users + demo posts + demo hunts.

DELIVERABLES
Working Laravel app in Replit with Postgres.
Migrations, models, controllers, policies, middleware.
Blade templates using Tailwind + Alpine.js.
.env.example with the required keys (weather, tides, species info) and clear instructions.
README.md with:
  - setup steps
  - demo accounts (admin/mod/member)
  - how to run migrations/seed
  - how to add API keys
  - explanation of provider abstraction and demo mode
Include a minimal test suite (feature tests for auth, permissions, post creation, hunt generation in demo mode).

BUILD PLAN (EXECUTE)
1) Scaffold Laravel + Breeze (Blade) + Tailwind + Alpine.
2) Configure Postgres connection, migrations, seeders.
3) Implement roles/permissions + admin UI.
4) Implement posts/images/feed/comments/likes/reports.
5) Implement hunts + checklist + provider interfaces + demo mode + queue/caching.
6) Polish UI and add badges.
7) Add tests + README.

Now implement the full app accordingly. Do not skip steps or leave stubs; if an external API is not available without keys, implement demo mode with realistic sample responses.
